name: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023
description: International standard for systems engineering lifecycle processes. Establishes a common framework for describing the lifecycle of systems created by humans, including conception, development, production, utilization, support, and retirement. Covers agreement, organizational project-enabling, technical management, and technical processes.
license_note: Metadata only. No ISO copyrighted text reproduced. Process descriptions are original summaries.

items.title: Acquisition process
items.description: Obtains a product or service in accordance with the acquirer's requirements. Covers need identification, supplier selection, agreement preparation, and oversight of delivery against agreed terms.
items.purpose: Obtain systems or services that satisfy acquirer requirements.

items.title: Supply process
items.description: Provides a product or service to an acquirer that meets agreed requirements. Covers proposal preparation, agreement execution, product delivery, and ongoing support arrangements.
items.purpose: Provide a system or service to the acquirer meeting agreed requirements.

items.title: Life cycle model management process
items.description: Defines, maintains, and assures the availability of policies, processes, models, and procedures for use by the organization. Establishes the set of organizational process assets.
items.purpose: Manage organizational lifecycle models and process assets.

items.title: Infrastructure management process
items.description: Provides the enabling infrastructure and services to support projects throughout the life cycle. Includes development tools, test environments, facilities, and computing resources.
items.purpose: Provide and maintain enabling infrastructure for system lifecycle activities.

items.title: Portfolio management process
items.description: Initiates and sustains necessary, sufficient, and suitable projects to meet the strategic objectives of the organization. Balances resource allocation across the project portfolio.
items.purpose: Manage the project portfolio aligned with organizational strategy.

items.title: Human resource management process
items.description: Provides the organization with the necessary human resources and maintains their competencies. Covers workforce planning, skills development, training, and knowledge transfer.
items.purpose: Provide and develop competent human resources for systems engineering.

items.title: Quality management process
items.description: Assures that products, services, and implementations of processes meet quality objectives and satisfy stakeholders. Defines quality policies, objectives, and methods for evaluating quality.
items.purpose: Assure products and processes meet quality objectives.

items.title: Knowledge management process
items.description: Creates, maintains, and provides access to organizational knowledge assets. Enables capture, sharing, and reuse of knowledge from systems engineering experiences and lessons learned.
items.purpose: Manage organizational knowledge for reuse and learning.

items.title: Project planning process
items.description: Produces and communicates effective and workable project plans. Covers scope definition, work breakdown, effort estimation, scheduling, resource allocation, and budget planning.
items.purpose: Create effective project plans to guide systems engineering work.

items.title: Project assessment and control process
items.description: Determines the status of the project and ensures project performance conforms to plans. Monitors progress, identifies variances, and takes corrective actions when needed.
items.purpose: Monitor project progress and take corrective actions when needed.

items.title: Decision management process
items.description: Provides a structured, accountable approach for selecting the most beneficial course of action from alternatives. Uses defined criteria, evaluation methods, and stakeholder input for decision making.
items.purpose: Support structured decision making for technical and management choices.

items.title: Risk management process
items.description: Identifies, analyzes, treats, and monitors risks continuously throughout the system lifecycle. Maintains a risk register and ensures risks are managed within acceptable tolerance levels.
items.purpose: Manage risks throughout the system lifecycle.

items.title: Configuration management process
items.description: Establishes and maintains the integrity of all identified work products of a project and makes them available to concerned parties. Manages configuration identification, change control, and status accounting.
items.purpose: Maintain integrity of work products through configuration control.

items.title: Information management process
items.description: Provides relevant, timely, complete, valid, and confidential information to designated parties. Manages information items, records, and documentation throughout the lifecycle.
items.purpose: Manage information items and records throughout the system lifecycle.

items.title: Measurement process
items.description: Collects, analyzes, and reports data to support effective management of processes and demonstrate quality. Covers definition of measurement objectives, collection procedures, and analysis methods.
items.purpose: Support management through measurement data collection and analysis.

items.title: Quality assurance process
items.description: Provides assurance that work products and processes comply with plans and applicable standards. Covers quality evaluations, audits, reviews, and corrective actions for quality findings.
items.purpose: Assure compliance of work products and processes with quality requirements.

items.title: Business or mission analysis process
items.description: Defines the business or mission problem or opportunity, characterizes the solution space, and determines a preferred solution. Covers concept exploration, stakeholder identification, and feasibility assessment.
items.purpose: Define the business problem and determine a viable solution approach.

items.title: Stakeholder needs and requirements definition process
items.description: Defines stakeholder requirements for a system that can provide capabilities needed by users and other stakeholders. Elicits, analyzes, and documents stakeholder needs as measurable requirements.
items.purpose: Define measurable stakeholder requirements based on identified needs.

items.title: System requirements definition process
items.description: Transforms the stakeholder requirements into a technical view of a required product that could deliver the desired services. Specifies functional, performance, and design constraint requirements.
items.purpose: Transform stakeholder requirements into system technical requirements.

items.title: Architecture definition process
items.description: Generates architecture alternatives, evaluates them against requirements and stakeholder concerns, and selects one or more for further development. Produces architecture descriptions and views.
items.purpose: Define the system architecture addressing requirements and stakeholder concerns.

items.title: Design definition process
items.description: Provides detailed data and information about the system and its elements sufficient to enable implementation. Creates design descriptions for all system elements with interfaces and interactions defined.
items.purpose: Create detailed design sufficient for system element implementation.

items.title: Implementation process
items.description: Realizes a specified system element, producing hardware, software, or other system artifacts. Covers fabrication, coding, assembly, and preparation of implementation artifacts.
items.purpose: Realize system elements through construction and fabrication.

items.title: Integration process
items.description: Assembles a system from its constituent elements consistent with the architecture and design. Progressively combines elements, verifying interfaces and interactions at each integration level.
items.purpose: Assemble and verify system elements into a complete system.

items.title: Verification process
items.description: Provides objective evidence that a system or system element fulfills its specified requirements and characteristics. Uses testing, analysis, inspection, demonstration, or review methods.
items.purpose: Verify that the system meets specified requirements.

items.title: Transition process
items.description: Establishes a capability for providing services in the operational environment. Covers installation, user training, data migration, and handover from development to operations.
items.purpose: Transition the system into its operational environment.

items.title: Validation process
items.description: Provides objective evidence that the system in its operational environment fulfills stakeholder requirements and achieves its intended use. Confirms the right system has been built.
items.purpose: Validate that the system fulfills stakeholder needs in its operational context.

items.title: Operation process
items.description: Uses the system to deliver its intended services. Covers operational procedures, performance monitoring, operator support, and management of the system in its operational environment.
items.purpose: Operate the system to deliver intended services.

items.title: Maintenance process
items.description: Sustains the capability of the system to provide a service. Covers corrective maintenance (fixing defects), adaptive maintenance (environment changes), perfective maintenance (enhancements), and preventive maintenance.
items.purpose: Maintain the system's capability through modifications and corrections.

items.title: Disposal process
items.description: Ends the existence of a system element or system. Covers decommissioning planning, data preservation, environmentally responsible disposal, and transitioning users to replacement systems.
items.purpose: Safely retire and dispose of systems no longer needed.
